Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: Let's go one step deeper on this and begin to look at other aspects of the radar echoes currently detected. If you select any of these echoes in the Tactical Display, you will see that their speed, course, and size are indicated.

As another example, a 'Very Large' radar echo transiting on a constant bearing that lines up with a known sea lane or transit corridor, maintaining a steady speed of 8-12 knots, is very likely a merchant ship. Here in the North Pacific, most of the merchant traffic we will encounter will be larger ships designed for long transit across the Pacific. Smaller coastal traffic will (as a rule) not be present. In some cases we may encounter fleets of trawlers heading to fishing grounds. More on that later!
